item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very wholesome! These kinds of scips make me wish I could work for the foundation (if it was real), but then i remember the spooky ones.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Pleasant" was definitely what we were going for here. Thanks for the comment and the +1, we really appreciate it! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what a pleasant read. i love scps like these that are heartwarming! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it! And thanks for reading over this in chat! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. Has early site "Weird thing" vibes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, I like the sound of "Zibes" (or "Zybes"?), but it doesn't really immediately sound like both of the words… they're not really easily combinable.

At any rate, I'm glad you liked the article! Thanks for the +1. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We really need a term for the good vibes that Zyn and co-authors make the readers feel in every SCP they write (myself excluded, ours was rather grim).

Zibes? Zynibes? I'll figure something out. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank for the nice comment and the upvote! :) It was Derps's idea to have the table be capable of bringing people together through music, and that was a big part of what made me excited to co-author this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to say I recall reading a very early draft about this either in chat or in the forums a few months ago. Back then I want to say the main focus was on the beings in the table, and I think moving the focus to the people who are listening was a good move, as it allows you to do a lot more with the characters and the little bits of personality present in the various foundation researchers.

Honestly, Zyn has these kinds of objects down to a science (do ho ho).

+1. Neat skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you enjoyed the read!

Have a dancing birb: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5sSb1qF7m3w

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the nice comment! We were trying to go for an old-school "could do some collab logs" kinda feel here, which readers could relate to in a "I wonder how it would react to me?" kinda way.

Thanks for the +1, my dude

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yee! That makes me happy. :D Thanks for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was refreshingly wholesome. I really enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Every once in a while you find one of those sweet skips that isn’t grimdark or even just plain strange. This is one of those and a pretty well written one at that!

Take my +1 you bastards

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really can't overstate how much I love seeing the occasional good vibes skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the upvote!

Have a dancing birb: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CEQuDyuQFKE

note: this video is what inspired that Elvis log at the end

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Music is my shit. Take an upvote.

item.title: Re: An Author post i guess
item.content:encoded:
This has been quite the journey! As a note, Derps picked 4117 because it's been 117 days since we initially started the collab sandbox for this work.

ETA: of course, big, big thanks to everyone who read this in chat! We really appreciate your time and enthusiasm. :D

I remember seeing the first draft in the forums and really liking the idea of a musical table connecting people through music. Basically being like the ultimate wingman, but for friends and not just dates.

As someone who generally prefers to eat alone, but also has a lot of great memories of eating meals with a bunch of friends (usually takeout, lol) I feel like it's important to have people you can just chill with sometimes. Just good to know that there's someone out there who might be thinking of you sometime in their days, when they hear that one song.

item.title: An Author post i guess
item.content:encoded:
This SCP was co-authored between me and Zyn, big thanks to my wonderful co-author, for putting up with my terrible schedule, and generally making the SCP what it is now compared to when I first posted it. Also, thanks to our reviewers in chat, theduckman, not_a_seagull, DarkStuff, Coryn02, taylor_iOStkin, and Pratten.

I came up with this idea when walking. Not much to talk about there, it's when I come up with most ideas.
